patch.value: Split search.ts (3119 LOC, 92% of 3400 cap) into rendering / query-parsing / inline-field modules

2026-07-04 code-quality sweep: src/cli/commands/search.ts is 3119 LOC vs the 3400 static-gate cap — one feature-add from failing the gate — and contains 4 sub-threshold jscpd clones internally (plus a cross-file clone with list.ts:489). Split along rendering vs query-parsing vs inline field:value syntax boundaries (the two parse layers from PR#290) to pay down both LOC and duplication.
search.ts split into rendering, query-parsing, and inline-field modules each under the LOC cap; coverage stays at 100 and search output byte-identical on golden queries.

## Context
2026-07-04 code-quality sweep: src/cli/commands/search.ts is 3119 LOC against the 3400 static-gate LOC cap — one feature away from failing the gate — and carries 4 internal jscpd clones plus a cross-file clone with list.ts:489.

## Approach
Split along the natural boundaries that already exist in the file: (1) result rendering, (2) query parsing, (3) inline field:value syntax (the second parse layer from PR#290 — keep the two layers' order intact). Follow the established split playbook (PR#396 cli-contracts, 2026-05-25 all-src <2000 LOC pass): extract modules at the same call position, keep the command's public surface byte-identical, and update vitest.config.ts excludes/mirrored test paths if files move.

## Definition of done
search.ts well under the cap (~≤1500 LOC per module); internal clones gone (jscpd clean at current minTokens); search output byte-identical on a golden query set; coverage gate still 100%.

author: codex-gpt5
message: Extract shared metadata filter matching from oversized search/list commands as part of broad documentation-contract PR

value.author: codex-gpt5
value.text: Implementation evidence: search rendering extraction remains in the broad docstring bundle; shared list/search timestamp predicates removed the final cross-command clone without changing filter semantics. pnpm typecheck, pnpm lint (0 clones), and focused search/list regression suites passed.

value.author: codex-gpt5
value.text: Scope boundary: this PR extracted search rendering and shared list/search timestamp filtering, reducing duplication to zero, but query-parsing and inline-field extraction remain outstanding and the acceptance target of <=1500 LOC per module is not yet met. Returning the item to open so only actively worked items remain in_progress.

author: codex-gpt5
message: Pause remaining search decomposition after broad docstring bundle

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Scope update after PR#506 (2026-07-11): the rendering slice is PARTIALLY done — search-rendering.ts extracted (137 lines) but search.ts is now 3740 physical / ~3330 effective lines (the static gate now counts only non-empty non-comment lines, changed in PR#506 to avoid penalizing docstrings — title's "3119/3400" numbers predate both changes). Effective count sits at ~98% of the 3400 cap, so this file is still one feature away from failing the gate. Remaining scope per PR#506 tracker note: query-parser + inline-field decomposition (keep the two parse layers' order from PR#290 intact). Suggested order: (1) inline field:value layer out first — it is the most self-contained and owns the list.ts:489 cross-file clone site; (2) query parser; (3) sweep remaining render helpers into search-rendering.ts.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Scope refresh 2026-07-13 (review pass #78): search.ts is now 3359 gate-normalized LOC (98.8% of the 3400 cap) — grown from the 3119 (92%) in this item's title. ~40 lines of headroom; any non-trivial search feature lands over the cap. Split urgency now on par with main.ts (see pm-yh8r census note of same date).
